title: Cyclic Homogeneous Oscillation (CHO): Auto-correlation Based Detection of Non-sinusoidal Neural Oscillations in ECoG, EEG, and SEEG

sections.name: Abstract
key_points: FFT-based methods conflate harmonics of non-sinusoidal oscillations with independent rhythms
CHO defines three criteria: peaks over 1/f in time and frequency, at least two full cycles, auto-correlation-confirmed periodicity
Evaluation on synthetic 1/f + non-sinusoidal bursts (2.5 cycles, 1--3 s) and on ECoG (8 subjects), EEG (7 subjects), resting-state ECoG (6 subjects), and SEEG hippocampal recordings (6 subjects)
At physiological SNR (-7 dB EEG, -6 dB ECoG), CHO detects 50--60% of true oscillations with far higher specificity than FOOOF, OEvent, SPRiNT
Resting-state: 11 Hz 450 ms auditory, 7 Hz 450 ms motor, 17 Hz Broca's beta, 8 Hz 450 ms hippocampal

sections.name: Introduction
key_points: Neural oscillations coordinate inter-regional activity and index brain states
Three questions: the when (onset/offset), where (spatial generators), what (fundamental frequency)
Non-sinusoidal oscillations generate FFT harmonics that contaminate peak detection
Illustrative auditory-cortex example: 1:3 phase-phase coupling confirms 18 Hz is harmonic of 6 Hz
Phase-phase coupling is computationally prohibitive for real-time/closed-loop use
CHO combines parametric 1/f-driven bounding boxes (extending OEvent) with auto-correlation periodicity testing
Three CHO criteria enumerated; comparison planned against FOOOF, OEvent, SPRiNT

sections.name: Related Work
key_points: Frequency-domain detection: FFT, FOOOF, specparam -- separate periodic from 1/f aperiodic component
Time-domain detection: Hughes et al. 2012, Gips et al. 2017 -- but assume sinusoidality
Time-frequency methods: Chen et al. 2011; SPRiNT (Wilson et al. 2022); OEvent (Neymotin et al. 2022)
Non-sinusoidal waveform literature: Belluscio 2012, Cole & Voytek 2017/2019, Donoghue 2022
Gap: no real-time-capable method jointly handles non-sinusoidality, bounding boxes, and periodicity

sections.name: Methods
key_points: CHO three-criterion pipeline (1/f thresholding, two-cycle rejection, auto-correlation periodicity)
Bounding-box merging: overlap >75% in time, neighboring in frequency
Four windows / 250 ms per 1/f spectrum; accurate detection if |ground truth peak - detected| < 1.5 Hz
Phase-phase coupling via Belluscio et al. 2012 n:m method
Subjects: ECoG x1-x8 (8, 4 females, 41+/-14 yr, avg IQ 96+/-18 range 75-120); EEG y1-y7 (7 males, 27+/-3.6 yr); resting-state ECoG ze1-ze8 (N=6); SEEG zs1-zs6 (6, 3 females, 46+/-16.6 yr)
Acquisition: BCI2000, 8x 16-ch g.USBamp EEG, 256-ch g.HIamp ECoG, Nihon Kohden JE-120A SEEG; sampling 1200 Hz EEG/ECoG, 2000 Hz SEEG; downsample to 400/500 Hz
Preprocessing: 2nd-order Butterworth HP at 0.05 Hz; common-mode reference excluding >10x MAD 60 Hz channels; polyphase antialiasing resample
SEEG electrodes: Pt-Ir, 2 mm x 0.8 mm, 3.5--5 mm spacing; ECoG: 4 mm (2.3 mm exposed) Pt-Ir, 10 mm center-to-center
Task: auditory reaction time to 1 kHz tone; warning tone below 100 ms; pre-stimulus 1.5 s analysis window; ECoG ~400 trials/subject; EEG 200 trials/subject

Anatomical: CT co-registered with MRI; Coon et al. 2016 for electrode localization; Adamek et al. 2022 VERA for Brodmann assignment; FreeSurfer (Fischl 2012) for cerebral region labeling; EEG 10-10 system

sections.name: Results
key_points: Synthetic 1/f bursts: CHO specificity > 0.7 across SNR vs SPRiNT < 0.3
Physiological SNR EEG -7 dB, ECoG -6 dB: CHO sensitivity comparable to SPRiNT
CHO higher overall accuracy than FOOOF, OEvent, SPRiNT for peak frequency detection
Onset/offset: CHO > OEvent specificity
ECoG pre-stimulus: CHO finds focal beta only in pre-motor; FOOOF finds beta broadly; Wilcoxon p<0.05 Bonferroni in most regions except precentral / pars opercularis / caudal middle frontal
EEG: CHO alpha entropy 3.82 < FOOOF 4.15 (max 4.16); beta entropy CHO 4.05 < FOOOF 4.15

Onset/offset auditory cortex: 7 Hz oscillations; offset peak 300 ms post-stimulus (200 ms post-response); onset re-emergence 950 ms post-stimulus; de-synchronized 250 ms / re-synchronized 900 ms post-stimulus on average
SEEG hippocampus: primarily alpha band; beta band close to alpha (8--14 Hz)

Resting-state frequencies: auditory (BA 41/42) 11 Hz 450 ms; motor (BA 4) 7 Hz 450 ms with >500 ms beta; Broca's area 17 Hz beta; hippocampus 8 Hz 450 ms, shorter than motor cortex alpha/beta (p<0.05, Wilcoxon rank-sum, N=6)

sections.name: Discussion
paragraphs: High specificity from three criteria (1/f threshold, two-cycle rejection, auto-correlation)
Focal motor beta and implication that auditory beta in conventional methods are theta/alpha harmonics
Hippocampal harmonics -- alpha-beta continuum (8-14 Hz) and implications for wide-band analysis
Onset/offset applications in closed-loop neuromodulation and neurofeedback (battery/power savings, phase-locked stimulation)
Limitations (sensitivity at low SNR, wavelet alternatives, state-space/EMD extensions) and pathological extensions (Parkinson's beta asymmetry, seizure-onset zone harmonics)

sections.name: Conclusion
key_points: CHO resolves when/where/what for non-sinusoidal neural oscillations
Supports closed-loop BCI, neurofeedback, and biomarker discovery

table_plan.caption: Comparison of oscillation detection methods on synthetic non-sinusoidal bursts at physiological SNR levels (EEG -7 dB, ECoG -6 dB)

figure_plan.caption: CHO three-step pipeline: 1/f thresholded bounding boxes, cycle-count filter, auto-correlation periodicity test
Predominant fundamental frequencies and durations across cortical regions during resting state
Spatial specificity (entropy across 64 EEG channels, lower = more focal) for alpha and beta bands
Onset/offset of 7 Hz oscillations relative to auditory stimulus in ECoG reaction-time task: desynchronization ~250 ms post-stimulus, resynchronization ~900 ms post-stimulus
